title: Schedule a welcome email 24 hours after signup via schedule_in
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.register_user/1` that takes a params map. Use `Ecto.Multi` to (1) insert a `User` changeset and (2) enqueue `MyApp.Workers.WelcomeEmailWorker` with `schedule_in: 86_400` (24 hours) using the newly-inserted user's id. This uses schedule_in, not cron, because the interval is per-user. Use string-keyed args. Return `{:ok, user}` or `{:error, changeset}`. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Must recognize 'X hours after event' is schedule_in, not cron. One-hop decision.
research_citation: Sorentwo 'Oban Recipes Part 3: Reliable Scheduling' — 'Delivering around the same time using cron-style scheduling would need extra book-keeping… The recursive scheduling approach is more accurate.'
